Manage opt-in anonymous usage telemetry

Usage:
  nectar telemetry [--endpoint <url>] <subcommand>

Arguments:
  subcommand          `enable`, `disable`, or `status`

End of options:
  Use `--` before positional values that start with `-`, for example `nectar new post -- --config`.

Options:
  --endpoint <url>    Set the stored telemetry endpoint when enabling. NECTAR_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT overrides it per run
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .nectarrc > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --endpoint → NECTAR_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  nectar telemetry status
  nectar telemetry enable
  nectar telemetry enable --endpoint https://telemetry.example.test/v1/usage
  NECTAR_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT=http://127.0.0.1:8787/usage nectar build
  nectar telemetry disable
